---
title: "A performance budget for a static blog"
description: "Set a budget, measure it, stick to it: performance as a design constraint."
pubDate: 2026-06-10
tags: ["performance", "astro"]
draft: false
series: "Modern Web Architecture"
seriesOrder: 2
---

A static blog has no excuse for being slow. Here is a reasonable budget and how to stick to it.

## The budget

| Resource | Budget | Rationale |
| --- | --- | --- |
| HTML | < 30 KB | Brotli-compressed content |
| CSS | < 20 KB | Purged Tailwind |
| Initial JS | < 50 KB | Islands only |
| Fonts | < 120 KB | 2 variable fonts, woff2 |
| LCP | < 1.5 s | On simulated 4G |

## Variable fonts

```css
@font-face {
  font-family: "Inter Variable";
  font-display: swap;
  src: url("/fonts/inter.woff2") format("woff2-variations");
}
```

A single variable font replaces four static weights. With `font-display: swap`, the text stays visible while the font loads.

## Images

```bash
# Astro optimizes at build time
bunx astro build
```

Astro's `<Image>` component generates AVIF/WebP with explicit `width` and `height`: zero layout shift.

## Measure continuously

Lighthouse in CI on every pull request, with a blocking threshold at 95. A budget that is not enforced automatically always ends up blowing past its limits.
